_meta._comment: Sort patch notes by: 1. Type (added → changed → fixed), 2. mod, 3. text. Keep patch notes simple and concise. Do not have duplicate mod+type entries — merge them into one.

changes.text: Quest access tiles (NPCs, holes, ladders, levers, fishing, and digging) now show a pulsing tile highlight (opacity 0–50%) when the board is empty; the effect is removed once creatures are placed, matching the Tesha tutorial arrow behavior. Highlighted pickup tiles use a right-click context menu before granting the item. A compact tutorial tooltip (game frame styling) appears above the first such tile. An optional side mission can be accepted and completed in parallel with the main King Tibianus chain.

Mission progress sync waits for the player name and retries on player state so fresh sessions hydrate from Firebase reliably. Arena leaderboard entries are removed when completed mission count is zero; full quest reset also clears the leaderboard row.
Quest tile highlight overlays now match scaled tile size at all zoom levels, refresh when quest inventory changes, and show a pointer cursor on the highlight hitbox.
Seamless autoplay now automatically unchecks the session widget's Stop after a defeat option so defeats are handled by the mod instead of stopping the session.
Fixed panel z-index (150) so the panel stays above Autoseller's floating auto badge (100) but behind native right-click context menus (z-modals = 200).
Fixed floating auto badge z-index (100) so Hunt Analyzer and Awaken Tracker panels can stack above it while both stay below native game context menus.

Fixed automation stalling on active tasks when the quest cooldown expired: in-progress hunts (gameId set) now take priority over new-task acceptance, so navigation and autoplay resume instead of looping on skipped accept attempts.

Fixed panel z-index (150) so the panel stays above Autoseller's floating auto badge (100) but behind native right-click context menus (z-modals = 200). Updated autoplay session timer parsing for the new Session widget (with and without run count).

Fixed intermittent mod load failures where only default bundled mods ran: registerLocalMods responses now use the message format local_mods expects, the init fallback timer is cancelled when stored states arrive, and a post-batch catch-up loads any enabled mods missed during a race with the fallback batch. Mod-button settings panels (Bestiary Automator, Board Analyzer, etc.) now stack above mod UI panels such as Awaken Tracker and Hunt Analyzer.

Tibia Ball League Event Competition: new Shiny tab tracks qualifying shiny Football Elementals per participant via profilePageData (Guilds-style rate limiting at 28 requests per 10s, 429 retries, and 1h profile cache). Rankings use shiny count with latest-drop tiebreaker (relative Drop time, e.g. 2h ago); only players with at least one shiny after the server-time cutoff (2026-07-17 00:00 UTC-3) appear on the board. Before tracking starts the summary timer shows red “Shiny starts in” instead of the raid end countdown; separate Shiny Beast Coin prize.

Hotkeys tab → Setups: configurable Auto-setup hotkey (default U) that clicks the game Auto-setup button using the same localization and wand-icon detection as Stamina Optimizer. Dropdown to load Setup 1–8 from Better Setups (default) or Setup Manager map shortcuts (F1 = first non-auto-setup saved setup; Auto-setup and matching saved setups stay on U). Loading a setup via hotkey (or Setup Manager shortcuts) shows a success toast with the setup name.

King Tibianus arena mission leaderboard (Firebase): rank from completed mission count, Quest Log card with tabbed Missions/Completed lists, shell-first modal loading, and completed-mission summaries with reward lines.

Tibia Ball League Event Competition modal opens immediately (no loading gate); Ticks, Rank, and Shiny tabs load on demand, reuse cached tab data when available, and prefetch inactive tabs in the background.
Content scripts and background injection now load only on /game and /pt/game (including www and subpaths). Other bestiaryarena.com pages no longer run the mod loader.

Related split quest lines are now treated as one quest with unified completion. Quest and encounter labels are consistent between the quest log and battle toasts. Objectives appear only when their prerequisites are met. Certain quest items remain in inventory only after the linked quest is finished. Mission list uses story order; scroll position is preserved when switching missions.

Renamed the built-in setup label from Original to Auto-setup (Autoconfigurar in pt-BR) to match the game UI. When a saved setup matches the game Auto-setup board, the standalone Auto-setup entry is hidden and matching setups show an (auto-setup) suffix and sort first in the modal and map shortcuts. Other saved setups can be reordered with up/down arrows. Saving a board that already matches a saved setup is blocked: Save is greyed out with a yellow warning icon (hover shows Duplicate setup). Setup card layout fixed so action buttons are no longer clipped. Creature count (e.g. 1/1 creatures) sits at the bottom of the action column. Modal size is 520×600.

Allies can no longer remain on villain spawn tiles: autoSetupBoard filtering, immediate boardConfig cleanup on placement or drag, and overlap removal before villain resync to prevent duplicate villains.
Firebase save/load reliability for merged quest lines: migration from older split-quest progress, persistence of mid-quest flags, and repair when progress was partially reset on refresh.

New optional quest content: Tibia-style NPC keyword chat, hand-in rewards, contextual map hints (hidden during battle setup or while allies are on the board), digging feedback, and multi-step follow-up missions. Right-click context menus on special tiles, direct loot pickup, levers, and shared sparkle VFX on pickups. New quest items include inspect descriptions. Custom-battle encounters use the Custom Battles system with configurable ally limits and Firebase progress saves.

sceneSpriteReplacements config on CustomBattle for encounter map visual overrides: swaps background tile sprite IDs inside #background-scene, scoped to #floor-below and tile-index decorations only (never #actors or outfit sprites), burst apply on room enter, and re-apply when battle starts after the scene DOM refreshes. Shared entry-setup helpers (scheduleEntryVillainSetup, runEntryVillainSetupIfNeeded, ensureCustomVillainsPresent) with configurable attempt delays; atomic removeOriginalVillains + add custom villains in one setState; shiny villain support.

Mobile long-press acts like right-click for mod context menus: platform.js installs a touch-hold shim on mobile WebExtensions (Orion iOS, Firefox Android, etc.) that fires a synthetic contextmenu after ~500ms when the finger stays still, so quest tile actions, teleporter rows, creature menus, and other right-click features work without a mouse. Cancels on scroll/drag, suppresses the follow-up ghost click, and optional short vibration. Desktop test override: sessionStorage ba-long-press-context (1 on, 0 off). Exposes BestiaryPlatform.prefersLongPressContextMenu() and installLongPressContextMenu().

Quest toasts use shared templates with color-coded variants and variant-based durations (empty results shortest, mission completions longest). Right-click context menus and tile interactions refactored for deduplication; fishing stays enabled across map switches. NPC chat modals share one dialogue cooldown (1s single-line, 2s per multi-line line, farewell closes 1s after the last reply). Puzzle progress resets when changing maps; cleared obstacles hide with the same poof as digging and restore on map leave. Some encounter map visuals use Custom Battles sceneSpriteReplacements instead of Quests-local DOM helpers. Late-stage quest access can be gated by Firebase flags separate from hand-in completion.

Sandbox custom battles no longer corrupt live fights: addVillains, removeOriginalVillains, preventVillainMovement, and ally-limit enforcement skip while gameStarted or serverResults is set, so creatures in #actors stay visible when battle starts. Ally creature right-click (and mobile long-press) is blocked during active custom battles in sandbox so Hero Editor cannot be used to cheat stats or equipment; villains and tile interactions are unaffected. Exposes CustomBattles.isAllyContextMenuBlocked().

Quest modal portraits display at 106×64 to match tab buttons. Tile interaction right-click registers on obstacle sprites instead of the whole tile, fixing mis-clicks on overlapping adjacent tiles. Map switches no longer crash after clearing obstacles (sprites are hidden, not removed from React-owned DOM). Tile interactions activate correctly when accepting missions mid-session.

Modal keyboard shortcuts: Up/Down moves selection through the filtered map list (highlighted row scrolls into view), Enter travels to the selected map or the first visible match. Hotkey support opens the native teleporter via the board goto button and focuses search (window.__betterTeleporterOpen).

Cyclopedia map navigation now opens directly on the Maps tab with the target region and map selected, matching creature and equipment behavior. Right-click Cyclopedia context menu on map rows is only available when the Cyclopedia mod is enabled.

Maps tab no longer shows a half Home page (player profile and search) stacked above map content when navigating from Better Teleporter or switching tabs while the Home profile is still loading; Home tab layout updates now preserve display:none when the tab is inactive.
Hotkeys tab → Mods: configurable key to open Better Teleporter (default P; migrates saved Room Hopper binding on first load). Shows a warning when Better Teleporter is disabled.

Modal now resizes to 750×550 on the first frame when opened instead of waiting ~500ms for the search input placeholder; centering is preserved after width changes and the open animation is suppressed as soon as the teleporter structure appears. Title-only detection avoids enhancing Cyclopedia and other mod dialogs. Mutation observer no longer causes tab freezes from layout/sort re-entry loops. Context menu clicks work reliably (menu mounted inside the teleporter dialog) and the menu stays at 200px wide instead of inheriting the 750px dialog child rule. Row highlight syncs to your current map after sort/filter DOM changes so the first map (e.g. Sewers) no longer stays falsely selected. Reset fully clears native text search, region filter, hide raids, and column sort—including when the search box is already empty but filtered rows remain—and waits for all maps to return before restoring canonical region/map order from maps-database.js (fixes scrambled grouping after search + sort + reset). Clearing column sort restores native region headers instead of leaving them clustered with globally sorted rooms. Toggling Hide raids & events off while XP/Stam sort is still active re-shows raid and event rows that stayed hidden.

Right-click a map row to open a Cyclopedia context menu with the map name and every creature/equipment on that board as separate entries. Map (red), creature (green), and equipment (blue) use in-game frame button styling with separators between sections; each opens Maps, Bestiary, or Arsenal for that entry. Reset button clears region filter, hide raids, search, and column sort. Sort and filter preferences persist when you close and reopen the teleporter in the same session (defaults on mod init). Localized in en-US.json and pt-BR.json.

Equipment hover tooltips now show the item name only (tier and stat removed). Region filter dropdown min-width reduced from 140px to 120px. Map column width increased to 180px (from 69px/130px responsive).
Home tab loading state ("Loading Cyclopedia...") is now centered in the modal content area instead of sitting at the top-left.

New Super Mod: enhances the native map teleporter with a 750×550 layout and green activated title. Adds an XP/Stam column, 3-state sort on Map and XP/Stam, sticky region headers, raid (red) and dynamic event (green) row colors, inline region filter and Hide raids & events beside search, search keywords raid/raids and event/events (plus evento/eventos in pt-BR), and creature/equipment hover tooltips. Detects the teleporter by dialog structure instead of English-only strings so it works in Portuguese and other game locales; mod UI strings are localized in en-US.json and pt-BR.json.
Retired and unregistered from the mod loader; map browsing features are superseded by Better Teleporter. Room_Hopper.js remains in the repo for reference only.
Characters tab: pressing Esc no longer clears modal content while the Cyclopedia dialog is still open; fallback cleanup now runs only after the modal is actually closed or removed.

Tibia Ball League Event Competition now excludes Firebase admins from joining/submitting/standings and purges their Firebase event records (participants, scores/rank-scores, replays) while keeping a view-only experience. Floors 0/15 now persist and fallback correctly for both ticks and rank/rankTicks when filtered game-API leaders are ineligible. Admin UI polish: no personal/penalty baselines, no "no run yet" status lines, non-clickable floor section, and dedicated right-side Event Competition button with matching frame styling and animated label. On wcfield, rank and floor bar sections both update when changing floors.

Prevented accidental active-task deletion when a new task signal appears during delayed or stale state updates: active tasks are now preserved by default in the new-task accept flow and are only removed when creature filtering explicitly rejects the current task.

event-competition.js injected after custom-battles.js (injector, utility_injector, content_injector) and added to web_accessible_resources; Firefox manifest now also lists custom-battles.js for parity with Chrome. README, Wikitext, mod_loading_optimizations, and mod_development_guide updated.

Tibia Ball League Event Competition: modal now has Ticks | Rank tabs (Ticks default) with separate 500 Beast Coin prizes (icon after amount), Current Leader line, and auto-submit on victory; overall standings use total ticks or rank points across floors 0–15 (9600-tick penalty per missing floor) with records or rank-tick tiebreakers. Top #1–#3 players shown in an Excel-style comparison grid with gold/silver/bronze medal headers (Records on Ticks; Rank omits redundant Floors row); summary uses a 35/60 layout with improved centering and font size. After the event ends the timer reads “Event ended”, new submissions are blocked, and Firebase scores are retained for review. Floor bar polish: no duplicate You/leader row when you lead, consistent Time | Rank | Floor separators after refresh, Floor 0 counter without /max or parentheses, and floor numbers use in-game floor scale colors.

Event creatures now appear in creature pickers only when their raid map is currently active (from raid state + room monsters); inactive event creatures remain preserved in autosell/autosqueeze keep/sell lists so saved configs restore when events return.

Tibia Ball League Event Competition: Top players now always shows #1-#3 plus your own row only when you are outside top 3; floor 0 and 15 leaderboard fetches still bypass local rate limiting/cache so event highscores stay live from the game API.

STATIC_RAID_EVENTS was removed as a separate export; EVENT_TO_ROOM_MAPPING is now the single source of truth for static raids, and dynamic-event detection checks mapping keys directly (Raid Hunter derives event names from the same mapping).

Tibia Ball League (wcfield) Event Competition: join to submit floor PBs to Firebase (floors 1–14; floors 0 and 15 use game highscores). Floor bar tracks the selected ascension floor; modal shows every floor (you vs leader), raid timer, participants, 500 Beast Coins prize, and top-player standings with a 9600-tick penalty per missing floor in tiebreakers. Locked floors are greyscaled and not clickable; PBs show a toast and refresh the floor bar. Cached bundled Firebase reads and full en-US/pt-BR translations.
Equipment setup dropdown excludes Grey Tome and Rainbow Shield (not obtainable from exaltation chests); equipment-lua-export EXALTATION_CHEST_EXCLUDED kept in sync.
Football Elemental added to EVENT_CREATURES (World Cup event creature excluded from awakening, Autoscroller/Autoseller pickers, and shiny progress).
Bag Outfits progress max updated to 201.
Grey Tome added to EVENT_EQUIPMENT so BIS Equipments max stays 141 (World Cup event alternate of Spellbook of Ancient Arcana; Rainbow Shield still counts).
Floor tab sorts by floor gain first; when floor is tied, same-floor tick improvement is highest first.
Specific Map picker excludes raid and dynamic event maps via maps-database.

Modal keyboard shortcuts: Esc closes, Up/Down moves selection through the filtered map list (highlighted row scrolls into view), Enter teleports to the selected map or the first match when none is selected.
Patch Notes popup header adds left/right arrows to browse previous and next versions without closing the modal.
Removed unused helper paths and redundant mutation scanning, inlined stamina-cost wrappers, and trimmed duplicate cleanup calls to reduce overhead without changing tasking behavior.
Toolbar button now toggles the panel open and closed (matching Awaken Tracker and Better Analytics), instead of only opening it.
Right-click a map card for a View in Cyclopedia context menu (Cyclopedia mod must be enabled); opens the Maps tab on that map and closes the Highscores modal first.

Maps tab no longer shows a squashed layout on first navigation to a map (e.g. from Highscores View in Cyclopedia): stale region leaderboard fetch callbacks are ignored once a map is selected, map home-search navigation waits for the Maps list, and column layout relayouts after map stats render.

Modal UI polish: 400px max width, streamlined labels (Fetch Player / Replace Profile / Restore Original Profile), profile-first status copy, 110px status area, centered Status heading, and yellow note for hidden-creature limits. Player fetches are rate-limited to 400ms. While viewing an imported profile, a persistent toast shows a clickable bestiaryarena.com/profile link. Full en-US and pt-BR translations.
i18n no longer treats empty translation strings as missing keys (fixes raw locale keys appearing in UI when a translation value is intentionally blank).

Super Mod to test with another player's public arsenal in sandbox: fetch a profile by username, preview monster/equipment counts, then temporarily replace your local monsters and equips with one-click restore from an in-session backup. Automatically switches to sandbox play mode after a successful fetch. Hidden creatures are not imported. Setup Manager–style modal UI; mod bar button cycles colors while an imported arsenal is active.

Settings adds "Show setups with missing equipment" audit: opens a popup list of affected saved setups, sorted by region→map order, and clicking an entry navigates to that map. Settings and audit buttons show a Cyclopedia-style warning icon when any setup is missing gear; settings modal height increased to 500px and audit action moved below the new-label row.
Settings panel is mounted inside the Exaltation Chest modal tree again, so clicking controls no longer closes the modal and panel buttons/selects respond normally.
Maps tab Floor runs now sort time correctly when floors are equal, even if saved run data mixes string and numeric floor/tick values.

Maps tab region summary Speedrun row now shows world-record tick totals (season 2) after leaderboard data loads; WR values were previously stuck at — because the display updated before the DOM was built.

Load Setup for inventory scripts places all matching creatures and applies equipment with fallback (same item: tiers 5→1, then AD and HP) before skipping. Warning/error toasts use red text for 10s and report substitutions or missing gear; success uses the default 5s toast. Monster-missing and invalid-format errors show specific messages.

Maps tab floor runs show floorTicks in the Time column (falls back to total game ticks). Rows faster than your profile floor time or the floor world record show the same ⚠️ invalid-run warning as speedrun/rank (season 2).
Map search supports AND/OR operators (e.g. rook AND raid, venore OR carlin); hover the search bar for syntax help. Raid maps show a raid icon beside the map name in all tabs.

Modal cleanup clears the resize listener on any dismiss path (overlay, ESC, Close), cancels pending search debounce, always dismisses the loading dialog on fetch errors, and removes the toolbar button when the mod is disabled.
Firebase run upload and text export skip invalid floor runs (faster than your best or floor world record, level-1 creatures) using the same validation as Cyclopedia.

Skip runs over N ticks (0 to disable) cuts long runs at the tick ceiling and continues analysis without saving ticks or replay data for those runs. Skipped runs show as purple chart bars in a separate sort group, a live/results Skipped runs counter (hidden at 0), and are excluded from min/max/median win-time stats. Total Time, Avg Run Time, and Est. Remaining still update from wall-clock time while skips are active.

Creature ability tooltips and indexed ability search text no longer include lore quotes (blockquote blocks and preceding separators are stripped after render; metadata quote lines are filtered from search indexing).
Dismissing the running analysis modal during skip-only stretches restores it on the next status update (previously waited for a non-skipped run).
Awakened status follows level: below 50 is always normal, 51+ is always awakened, and level 50 can be either via the awaken toggle. Turning off Max Awakened Mode sets level to 50 and clears awakened.

Renamed from Highscore Improvements to Highscores (popup, button, and modal title; no icon). Summary tab (default) lists all maps in maps-database order with tick/rank/floor rows, Cyclopedia-style tab icons, and map search. Footer shows maps with room to improve, aggregated Improvements (ticks/rank/floor totals), and Your WRs counts. Explored maps sort before locked WR-only entries; cards with no personal data are dimmed. Stored 9600-tick timeouts display as 9600 ticks (not —); a valid speedrun below timeout implies 100% floor when floor is unset. Tick/Rank/Floor tabs include maps with world records even when uncleared. Sort: unlocked, own WR at bottom, tab tiebreaks (tick/rank gain desc, floor asc), then map order. Rows compare you → WR (holder) with green/red max caps on rank and floor; Summary marks your records as (You).

Summary and comparison rows show world records when your tick, rank, or floor is missing. Rank tab no longer claims max rank with zero rank runs; tick/floor tabs use accurate empty states when no WR data exists. Tick/Rank/Floor tab sort again prioritizes biggest improvement before map-order tiebreak. Mod cleanup closes the open modal and backdrop on disable.
Added buildMapOrderIndex, getMapOrderIndex, and compareMapsByGameOrder for canonical region→room map order.

Rankings tab keeps loading when the modal lifecycle aborts other in-flight requests (e.g. ESC or background modal cleanup). Rankings fetches are no longer tied to modal teardown; aborted loads retry while the table is still mounted.

World records re-fetch when you set a new personal best or finish a battle (including autoplay and sandbox), with a follow-up retry for slow backend updates. Post-battle refreshes bypass the same-map throttle so stale tick/rank/floor WRs no longer linger after a highscore. Rapid map switching now keeps up: map changes use trailing debounce instead of a 1s throttle, in-flight updates queue a retry for the latest map, and stale fetches are discarded if you move again before data returns.

Running progress overlay caps height at 320px (viewport-clamped); the body scrolls when content exceeds the limit while the header and Stop button stay fixed. Width and height limits live in MANUAL_RUNNER_RUNNING_MODAL_CONFIG (220–260px wide).

Autoplay Dragon Plant and Monster Squeezer show a centered green AUTO badge when autoplant or autosqueeze is enabled; fixed overlays follow each slot responsively (rAF, resize/mutation observers, viewport scroll). Clicking opens the Autoseller modal on the matching tab. Header nav icon copies Inventory responsive sizing (12×12 minimum).

AUTO badges no longer wrap autoplay buttons (fixes React removeChild crashes). Mod cleanup tears down badge trackers, debounce timers, injected styles, and globals with isCleaningUp guards so observers and rAF loops cannot run after disable.
Chat header icon copies Quest/Store responsive sizing (12×12 minimum), synced from native nav icons when the bar is built or refreshed.
Header nav icon scales up when the label is hidden (same responsive pattern as Inventory and Challenges).

Multiplayer tab uses the same three-column layout as Solo (map/summary, creatures, leaderboard). Pulsing queue-waiting header opens Multiplayer with a subtle colored pulse; help panel section titles and body text are slightly larger. Multiplayer in-battle victory modal shows opponent and a waiting-for-score message instead of solo global rank; final match outcome uses a win/lose/draw modal with both scores. Removed unused assets/challenges art and manifest/README references.
VIP Chat header button icon scales up when the label is hidden (same responsive pattern as Inventory and Challenges).

Multiplayer Join queue footer works again after the layout refactor (handlers registered when the queue panel is created). Fixed false win-by-disconnect after a normal loss or when the opponent finished first; completed matches stay in Firebase briefly so both clients read final scores; forfeit is not shown after your score is submitted. Score toasts and result modals no longer swap player names when sorted matchId keys differ from join order. Board resets after closing victory/defeat OK (custom villains removed, Stop, room reloaded). Multiplayer victories no longer save to the solo leaderboard. Rahemos is excluded from random creature rolls.

Player online highscore no longer overwrites Firebase with a lower peak when the latest remote record could not be fetched (e.g. transient read failure); updates are skipped until a successful read confirms the current record.

Room Hopper modal resized to 900×700 (viewport-clamped), region filter now follows true game region order, and the room list gained sticky per-region headers in default view. Table layout was refined (Room 250px, XP/Stam 100px, Equipments 200px), map names wrap instead of ellipsis, creature portraits fill available space before +N, and sortable headers are centered with 3-state sorting (Room: asc/desc/default, XP/Stam: desc/asc/default) that restores default grouped sticky regions.

Playtime no longer ticks on init, reload, or idle time before hunting: the live clock stays at accumulated history (or 00:00:00 on a fresh start) until the first battle starts (newGame), then baselines sync so pre-battle idle is excluded.

Extension popup fills the full mobile sheet again (Firefox Android, Orion iOS): no 600px height cap on phones, ignores the 280×280 first-paint stub with rAF retries and visualViewport sizing, and mobile CSS uses 100% width/height with safe-area padding instead of fixed 600×600 / 280px min caps.

Characters tab (Player Information + Leaderboards) now stacks columns vertically on narrow modal widths and returns to side-by-side on wider layouts. Leaderboards uses a shared dual-column scroll flow again, player search box content is centered/auto-fit, and search validation now shows a red X for invalid/no-data hits (including placeholder profiles) and a green check only for valid results.

Start, pause, and Skip board buttons are found more reliably: structure-based detection for the sm:order-3 control row ([Start/Iniciar] + Manual/Autoplay/pause), pause is clicked when autoplay blocks Start, Skip matches the lone button under sm:order-3 (including stamina-cost skips with data-full="true"), and loading Auto / Parar / Skip labels are no longer mistaken for Start.

Popup responsive sizing no longer regresses on mobile after the Edge first-paint viewport fix: mobile popup dimensions now always follow the real viewport size, while desktop Edge keeps the safeguard against locking to the initial stub viewport.

createModal with explicit height flex-fills the inner wrapper and .widget-bottom on creation. Mod development guide documents the standard responsive modal pattern: open via createModal/showModal, call setupModalResponsiveLayout immediately, first layout pass in requestAnimationFrame, resize listener until close. Do not defer sizing with setTimeout. Extension popup patch notes min height reduced by 50px (content area 200px).

Renamed database mod file from Welcome.js to welcome.js across mod registry, manifests, and popup lists. database/welcome.js always loads as infrastructure (loading toast and completion listener); welcome-enabled / Never Show Again only controls the welcome modal. Welcome modal rebuilt with Cyclopedia-style layout (dedicated scroll area, responsive sizing, thin scrollbars). Compact two-column feature grid; emoji replaced with official bestiaryarena.com icons; body text at 14px. Never Show Again and Got It! use the createModal footer. welcome.js reorganized into numbered sections 1–11; modal copy updated for current popup features.

New creatures and equipment now default to the Keep column when they are absent from both saved filter lists. Sell-all configs (empty keep list with a full sell list) no longer auto-sell, squeeze, or disenchant newly released content. Legacy keep-only configs, init migration, and runtime filter checks aligned so tri-state list logic is consistent in the UI and at game end.

Bundled mod dialogs no longer flash open at collapsed height before content fills the shell (4.3.2 responsive modals aligned with Challenges): Autoseller, Autoscroller, Better Boosted Maps, Better Forge, Better Rune Recycler, Better Setups, Better Tasker, Cyclopedia, Depot Manager, Dice Roller, Guilds, Hero Editor, Manual Runner, Mod Settings, Raid Hunter, Stamina Optimizer, Welcome; setup helpers use requestAnimationFrame instead of delayed apply. Extension popup opens at full 600×600 size in Edge (no longer clamps to 280px when the browser reports an uninitialized stub viewport on first paint).

database/welcome.js stays enabled in stored mod states after the Welcome.js rename (legacy disabled entries no longer skip the loading toast). Welcome modal no longer permanently skips first load when another game modal is open at completion time; ESC close is retried instead of aborting all later show attempts. Viewport resize now matches Cyclopedia: getWelcomeDialog falls back to open dialogs, setup no longer overwrites the resize cleanup, and window resize listener is removed on close. Orphan Close footer removed (buttons passed to createModal instead of an in-content footer with buttons: []).

Major in-file refactor (~21k lines): ModalLifecycle teardown (tab cleanups, abort in-flight fetches, modal-scoped handlers/observers); soft cache refresh on open (60s TTL, Maps TRPC cache preserved); lazy tab init. CyclopediaApi unifies profile, rankings, and TRPC. All tab factories hoisted (Maps, Bestiary, Arsenal, Inventory, Characters); shared render components and createStartPage in dedicated sections; openCyclopediaModal reduced to orchestration (~370 lines). File reorganized into sections 1–12 (Formatting, Home Search, tabs, modal, exports). Database helpers replace local REGIONS/ROOMS/equipment scans; fallback paths trimmed (gazer/awaken guesses, REGION_NAME_MAP, INVENTORY_CONFIG empty object, equipment ID brute-force). window.Cyclopedia.close closes modal only; full disable via cleanup. Maps: flexible region panel, spawn levels grouped with golden ×count multiplier.

Cross-tab creature links (Maps/Arsenal → Bestiary) select the creature on the first click (lazy tab page getter). Characters Rankings header/body alignment stable with scrollbars. Maps local-run tables no longer duplicate rows from async repopulation; Floors horizontal scrollbar corrected. Maps Creature Information shows spawn list again (not map metadata only).

When a map has per-map equipment rules, automation only starts if the daily boost matches one of those rules; no longer navigates or farms with generic fallback when the boosted equipment does not match.
Quest-log task removal no longer throws UI_TEXT is not defined — remaining Remove/Confirm/Suggested map lookups use getBothLanguages instead of removed UI_TEXT constants.
Settings modal max size is 530×500; on phones it auto-fits viewport width and height (desktop max unchanged). Main two-column panel fills remaining modal height and resizes on orientation change.
Settings modal max size is 770×500; on phones it auto-fits viewport width and height (desktop max unchanged). Main three-column panel fills remaining modal height and resizes on orientation change.
Settings modal max size is 700×500; on phones it auto-fits viewport width and height (desktop max unchanged). Main two-column panel fills remaining modal height and resizes on orientation change.

Settings panel max size is 500×500; on phones it auto-fits viewport width and height (desktop max unchanged). Opens beside the chest when there is room, otherwise below it and fills remaining viewport height. Equipment setup selectors keep usable minimum sizes; cramped rows scroll horizontally.

Settings modal max size is 620×500 (Arsenal 260px, Forge 320px); on phones it auto-fits viewport width and height (desktop max unchanged). Two-column panel fills remaining modal height and resizes on orientation change. Fixed missing Forge column border; Disenchant and Auto-upgrade tab content spans full column width.

Settings modal max size is 750×535; on phones it auto-fits viewport width and height (desktop max unchanged). Configuration and Statistics columns fill remaining modal height and resize on orientation change. Configuration rune grid expands to fill column height and scrolls when needed.

Load Setup and Settings modals max size is 350×410; on phones they auto-fit viewport width and height (desktop max unchanged). Settings label list and Load Setup textarea fill remaining modal height and scroll when needed.

Settings modal max size is 700×500; on phones it auto-fits viewport width and height (desktop max unchanged). Creature list fills remaining column height (expands when the unselected-creatures warning is hidden). Warning banner only shows when at least one creature is unchecked.

Purchase-order settings panel max size is 350×500; on phones it auto-fits viewport width and height (desktop max unchanged). Panel is attached to the shop dialog so clicks work when placed below; opens beside the Yasir shop when there is room, otherwise directly below it with scrollable tab content. Tab content fills remaining panel height and resizes on orientation change.

Arsenal equipment IDs no longer shift when duplicate items are in depot; hidden depot tiles are matched to depot rows before visible arsenal buttons are assigned.

Error Log in popup Debug section: removed Refresh button (panel still reloads when expanding Debug). Shortened description text.
Maps tab Top 5 Speedruns/Ranks/Floors tables refresh when RunTracker saves a run for the selected map (no longer stale until re-selecting the map).
Same-setup speedrun and rank entries no longer overwrite stored bests with slower or worse runs; only faster times or better rank scores update the row.

Error Log in popup Debug section: prepends a sticky Device / Browser header (extension version, browser, platform, device type, language, URL, user-agent, timestamp) that survives Clear and is included when copying. Header uses live page context when a bestiaryarena.com tab is active.
Early activation no longer throws ReferenceError for BETTER_SETUPS_TOAST_DURATION (constant moved before init calls showSetupNotification).

Firefox iOS / Orion / mobile WebExtensions: bundled mod loading fixed end-to-end. Shared extension-url.js encodes path segments for getURL/fetch/script injection (regex fallback when URL() rejects spaced paths). Logical paths and spaced filenames (Official Mods/, Turbo Mode.js) map to on-disk underscore folders/files via resolveBundledModPath; popup and storage keep spaced names. web_accessible_resources exposes underscore folder globs (mods/* alone missed nested WebKit subfolders). Injector content script loads mod text first (fetch + XHR); mobile relaxed loader tries page fetch, content-script bridge, then background getModContent; skips background existence probes that flooded Error Log. Firefox manifest loads extension-url.js in background; top-frame-only injection matches Chrome. ba-sandbox-utils.mjs and mod-coordination.mjs load as regular scripts (not type=module). Error Log includes failed resource URL in getModContent detail. Desktop Chrome/Firefox unchanged.
Firebase best-runs upload $replay region field uses canonical region names via maps-database (fixes Abdendriel vs Ab'Dendriel in uploaded solutions).
Stored run regionName uses maps-database resolution instead of title-casing the region id.

getRegionDisplayName() and getRegionDisplayNameFromRegion() prefer static REGION_NAME_MAP over game REGION_NAME / REGIONS[].name so replay and export region labels use canonical spellings (e.g. Ab'Dendriel instead of Abdendriel).

Error Log in popup Debug section: captures console errors, uncaught errors, unhandled rejections, mod load failures, and background mod fetch failures (warnings are not recorded). Persists across reloads (last 200 entries); Refresh, Copy, and Clear buttons. Useful for debugging on mobile without DevTools.
Popup: Debug Mode and Error Log moved from Extras into a dedicated Debug collapsible section (below Extras). Error Log records errors only — console.warn output is not stored or shown in the panel.

Full EN/PT localization (mods.awakenTracker): panel UI, tracker event logs and skip reasons, overview filters and badges, Farm Maps panel, and toolbar button. Clear All confirmation button now shows Confirm instead of Confirm clear all.

Creature picker and ID lookups use shared creature-database helpers (same gazer/event filter as Autoscroller, findMonsterByGameId/findMonsterByName). Merged duplicate quest-log creature extractors. Mod unload tears down subscriptions, timers, and coordination listeners; removed unused quest-blip helpers.

Mod load failures show one loader summary toast (failed mod count and names, auto-retry up to 3 with page refresh) from ui_components via local_mods — not duplicate Welcome error toasts. Success and loading toasts stay in Welcome.

Replaced tabs permission with activeTab (avoids Chrome "Read browsing history" wording). Background no longer queries the active tab — delivery uses contentScriptReady and sender.tab.id; popup passes tabId for toggles and run. Injects content/platform.js before client.js.

Unchecked creatures now remove the active task reliably: resolves from questLog.gameId and game state (not only quest-log DOM), runs during task hunting and on settings change, and matches checkbox keys. Autoplay pauses reliably at max task kills with session-aware detection, DOM retries, and no resume past quota.

Mobile WebExtensions (e.g. Orion iOS): relaxed loader when platform detects mobile or page extension fetch fails — background-first mod content, skip bulk HEAD probes, shorter page-ready wait, API wait fallback, hydration/game-state warnings non-fatal (no auto-refresh loop). Desktop strict loader unchanged. Optional override: sessionStorage ba-relaxed-loader (1 or 0).

Legacy personal bests (local profile after a dev reset — faster than the live WR or no public WR yet) show in red with a single ⚠️ icon and Legacy hover tooltip instead of the achievement icon; the public WR stays visible or shows — when empty. Time uses game.getTickHighscores. Refactored shared section config/render helpers; mod unload detaches context-menu listeners, clears pending timeouts/subscriptions, and ignores in-flight API updates.

"Always navigate max floor" now skips max-floor sync during Raid Hunter, Better Boosted Maps, Better Tasker, and Stamina Optimizer navigation so each mod keeps its configured floor; uses the same guard pattern as replay setups, with a re-check before the delayed floor apply.

Removed ineffective DOM query cache; kept WeakMap item-key memo only. Consolidated sell-quantity span styling and sell-row inventory updates. Mod unload and settings-panel close now detach shop input timers, PO dropdown listeners, and item-key cache.

Firebase run upload and local .txt export skip Cyclopedia "might be invalid" runs (faster than world record, faster than your best, worse rank than your best, level 1 creatures) and use the next valid entry instead; Season 2 world records are fetched once per upload.

Depot overlay and depot multiselector buttons show again when Depot Manager creature depot is enabled (removed an over-strict mod-registry check; filter panel rebuilds when depot controls are missing or stale). Those buttons stay hidden when Depot Manager is unloaded or creature depot is disabled.

Portuguese shop: sell section header now rewrites to Vender (game uses "dust" not "poeira" in the exchange title). Buy/sell table detection uses shared EN/PT header matching instead of scattered exact-string checks.

Author credit moved from the sell-modal activated title to the extension popup mod card (localized by megafuji link to profile). Sell-modal creature slots show Depot Manager favorite hearts; depot creatures are hidden from the grid by default. Overlays: Depot toggle shows them at 90% greyscale. Creature Selector: Depot multiselector uses custom selection (including locked creatures, Autoscroller-style green tile and thin white border, full color while selected) with footer Send N to depot / Remove N from depot buttons for bulk transfer; it turns off when you use sell presets or the game multiselect, and clears game selection when enabled.

Closing the sell modal with ESC or opening Cyclopedia from the right-click menu no longer crashes React (mod detaches observers on external close instead of removing injected DOM while the dialog unmounts).

Characters Rankings tab loads faster: background prefetch when browsing Characters, parallel API fetch while the table builds, qualified full-list cache for instant paging and sorting, and lighter batch row rendering with delegated player links. Opening from the Better Bestiary creature menu prepares and closes the sell modal (ESC sequence) before Cyclopedia opens so scroll-lock does not block or crash the UI.

Better Bestiary integration: right-click menu on sell-modal creatures adds Send to depot and favorite toggle (React creature ID resolution). Depot layout hides depot creatures in the modal grid as well as the main bestiary; optional show-depot overlay applies 90% greyscale (skipped on depot-multiselected slots). Bulk transferCreaturesDepot / remove APIs power multiselector footer actions. Debounced favorite-heart updates avoid freezing the backpack when favorites are enabled on large collections.

NEW MOD! Bestiary sell modal: Creature Selector presets (Already Shiny, Already Awakened, Already A/S) select unlocked duplicates when you already own that milestone per species; Overlays column toggles Maxed, Sealed, and Shinies temporarily (Mod Settings styling, restored on close); activated title and author credit in the details column. EN/PT labels and tooltips.

README and Wikitext document Better Bestiary (26 Super Mods). Popup EN/PT mod descriptions synced: Better Analytics sandbox, Better Forge type filter, Depot Manager, Room Hopper and Awaken Tracker locale keys.

Per-attempt console output is one summary line (result, seed, skip, esc/sell/inject, coordination waits). Perf logs show tab heap delta (+NMB) per batch; slim serverResults snapshots and per-attempt memory prune reduce long-run retention.

BetterUIApplyCreatureCosmeticsIn and incremental variant apply max, shiny, and sealed styling inside scroll-locked dialogs (Bestiary modal) with the same colors and Maxed > Shiny > Sealed priority as the main UI.

Level-up reward collection, cube opening, and daycare defer while the battle loot modal is open or Manual Runner / Board Analyzer is handling post-run rewards, so inject/sell/keep are not interrupted.

Dust counter reads inventory.dust (not context.dust), parses { count } dust objects, refreshes after disenchant and forge completion, and no longer shows 0, NaN, or stale values during multi-step auto-upgrade.

Loot handling: handlingRewardScreen coordination flag; skip waits for the reward modal; shorter wait when no creature drop; board idle reset after rewards. Stops with a clear message when stamina is insufficient (including board stuck after the last run with no potions). Auto-sell is skipped when inject already consumed the reward drop (same rule as Autoseller).
Loot and creature drop grids use tighter spacing: 2px grid and display flex gaps, 2px cell padding, 4px cell border radius, and 2px display padding.

Runs are captured once per battle via the network server-results path only; removed the board subscription re-process that could save the same win again after XP applied (e.g. level 12 then 13). Session dedupe by seed + mapId keeps battle-time creature levels in saved setups.
Arsenal Type filter (click to cycle All, Type, T1–T5). All keeps tier-high-first sort; Type and tier filters sort by equipment name, then tier high to low.

Equipment CDR source-of-truth exports: getEquipmentCdrPercentByGameIdMap() and getEquipmentCooldownReductionPercent(equipment), dynamically read from each equipment EffectComponent at the equipped tier (no hardcoded CDR values).

Pre-battle equipment cooldown reduction refactor: first render may show base CD, then board cards auto-refresh to tier-scaled CD after async EffectComponent parsing cache resolves (Cyclopedia-style mount/read path). Removed deprecated chunk/script CDR helper path and debug logging noise.
Attack speed buff tooltip scaling now uses full per-stat ratio (removed incorrect /2 halving), aligning passive math with in-game values (e.g., Deer AP scaling).

Arsenal equipment details: T1–T6 tier selector (default T5, resets when modal closes). T1–T5 use forge tiers; T6 previews Awaken Cyclops in-battle upgrade via data-rarity AWAKEN-CYCLOPS-TIER and createUIComponent { tier: 'AWAKEN-CYCLOPS-TIER' } (not numeric tier 6). Documented in game_state_api.md Equipment Data.
Arsenal equipment details column: 40%/55% row split (was 30%+10px/65%), top-aligned tier/portrait/description block, separator under T1–T6 buttons, tighter Found in maps/boosted lists spacing.
Equipment Data documents forge tiers T1–T5, Awaken Cyclops upgrade (AWAKEN-CYCLOPS-TIER string tier for effect tooltips), and Cyclopedia T6 preview behavior.

Equipment tier helpers (EQUIPMENT_TIER_STAT_BONUSES, clampEquipmentTier(), mountEquipmentEffectComponent() with { tier } for EffectComponent tooltips). EVENT_EQUIPMENT with isEventEquipmentName() and getBisProgressEquipmentNames() for Cyclopedia BIS progress.
Rune loot detection for Transfusion, Kaleidoscopic, and Conversion runes (name patterns and rune-shiny-transfer, monster-recycle, and conversion sprite keys).

Fight Units snapshot performance: per-actor incremental refresh, cached 661 passive-CD checks and cooldown-component lookups, resource-set signatures for status effects (not every DoT tick), and ability-tooltip fresh lookups no longer wipe the fight snapshot cache.

Pre-fight equipment cooldown-reduction preview scales with equipped tier (linear T5 max from chunk 235 constants; uses game scaleEquipmentEffect utils when available). Board preview ability CD for AP-scaled abilities (e.g. Slime) reads each monster's own script 661 CDR constants with equip −% base applied; passive abilities (e.g. Rat) no longer show a false timed CD from other creatures' constants. Open ability tooltips rescale cooldown reduction when equipment changes without reopening. Preview CD recognizes wakeCooldown, flurryCooldown, and similar; indirect metadata constructors (Bog Raider, Dwarf Geomancer) resolve ...t.skill spreads; no atk delay for non-auto-attack units (Bonelord); function-style magic auto-attacks (Elf Arcanist); addCooldown without skill spread (Ghoul Energysap); display-only abilityCooldown clocks (Goblin Assassin). Live Units atk delay and ability CD count down during manual/autoplay (engine tick, not frozen gameTimer). Temporary fight stat changes (e.g. Knightstatue stun zeroing AD/AP, +ARM) update Units cards via live stat bags and per-tick polling on the Units tab. Ability tooltip scaling: Orc Shaman revive DoT percent collapses parenthetical ratio; Rotworm Fishtail heal per AP chunk (e.g. 50 HP at 84 AP, not AP×chunk); no space before trailing punctuation. Unit stat display normalizes floating-point values. Units panel buff/debuff chips clear when a fight ends or sandbox is paused (only shown while combat ticks are actively advancing). Pre-fight atk delay and ability CD restored after Next.js migration: monster/equipment game scripts resolve from loaded webpack chunks (e.g. 876) via content markers with retry until available, equipment reuses the shared monsters chunk when both live in the same file, and units preview refreshes once (debounced) when scripts finish loading.

Wiki Lua export merges a fresh getEquipment() scan with ALL_EQUIPMENT and HARDCODED_BOOSTED_MAP keys so new game equipment is not omitted when equipment-database.js cached an older list. Wiki _Tiers block generated from equipment-database EQUIPMENT_TIER_STAT_BONUSES.

Units panel board-placement preview before fight: catalog-scaled attack delay (32-tick default when missing), ability CD from game script 661 (fixed inits, AP-scaled CDR, passive untimed abilities), equipment portraits on unit cards, and pre-battle ability CDR from equipment effects (chunk 235, e.g. Spellbook −20%). Board stats use game equipment-tier bonuses when utils load. Refreshes when scripts load or board/equipment changes.
Rune helpers for mods: runeKeysOrder and runeNonSacrificableKeys from categories/variants, plus getRuneType(), getRuneTypes(), and getRecyclableRuneTypes() (alias-aware via resolveItemKey).

Ability tooltip durations as ticks: AP-scaled chunks, cooldown spans, and CDR clauses with hover math — AP reduction, equip −% base in the displayed total reduction, effective CD, and minimum CD. Open tooltip scaling refreshes coalesced (100 ms debounce, 150 ms board poll) without console spam. Uses creature-database.js for creature name/metadata lookups, stat icons, and portraits (game-state fallback). Refactored battle-log summary and export formatting, subscription teardown, and UI override helpers — no intended behavior change.

Requires inventory-database.js with no inline fallbacks: rune list, sacrificable filter, recycling config, sacrifice building, validation, counts, and Created/Consumed stats all use the database helpers. Kaleidoscopic Rune from API alias runeRecycleMonster is normalized in Created stats.

Preview ability CD matches fight script logic; passive abilities (e.g. Rat) show no timed CD. Equipment CDR affects ability CD only, not attack delay. Equipped AP in pre-fight tooltips uses tier-table bonuses (capped, e.g. Deer 104 AP not inflated +24). Post-combat open tooltips settle on board stats, not fight buffs. Panel close and units grid rebuild tear down ability mounts, timers, and preview caches.

Ability tooltips scale to the selected unit's AP/AD/HP: flat and per-stat bonuses, inline speed and AP multipliers, duration chunks, lifesteal %, and cooldown reduction as total ticks with hover math (effective CD and minimum CD).

dumpCreatureWikiLua() exports obtainable combat stats at maxed level 50 and 99 via scaleStat ("50 (99)" when they differ); gazers and unobtainables use fixed stats. Added Larva and Scarab unobtainable map stats.

Battle log summary overkill counts post-mitigation excess only. Cooldown-reduction minimum uses ability script/constants (not in-fight effective CD); distant CDR const refs (e.g. Slime) resolved from the game bundle. Status bar stops refreshing with no new data after victory.

Fixed mod loader injecting on /abilities and other manifest-excluded pages: the background fallback now reads exclude_matches from the manifest at startup (update manifest.json and manifest_firefox.json only). Profile exclusion widened to profile* so bare /profile is covered.
Manual and Autoplay panel tick counter (tickEngine during fights; gameTimer stays 0). Victory/Defeat battle log end marker, status bar, and export final tick via onGameEnd winner and server results.
Toggle-off error tearing down manual play detection; board state subscribe/unsubscribe now supports XState v3 subscription objects.
Firebase run upload replay links when setup pieces stored numeric monster or equipment IDs instead of names.
Summary tab (Units | Battle Log | Summary) with live per-creature combat stats; export append includes matching team totals, DPS/HPS, damage share, summon instances, and overkill.

Summary allies and enemies shown side-by-side. Panel tick display uses gameTimer only (no poll); shared tick key for status bar, summary DPS, and export. Turbo-aware UI throttling, status bar render dedup, and 100ms summary refresh.

Autohide creatures on profile page (renamed; tooltip explains behavior) now hides non-shiny, non-awakened creatures and all gazers. Show genes and equipment on hover renamed from advanced stats. Ability hover tooltip max width set to 200px.
Combat summary roster vs summon detection; per-summon instance stats (#1/#2 + tile); died N× labels; zero-damage roster units included.

Battle log search below type filters (partial, exact "phrase", AND/OR syntax; filters first, then search; Esc clears). Full en-US and pt-BR panel translations for tabs, filters, status bar, log text, and export.
Slow motion row: speed % beside the title (hidden when off); Sandbox only inline warning outside sandbox; removed Game speed header, hint text, and Turbo Mode label.

Fixed automation closing unrelated menus and failing to accept/start tasks; duplicate accept and navigation runs; unreliable finish at 60/60. Quest-log close is scoped to the quest log, manual map changes pause automation, and completion retries on Finish failure.
Shortened long backup timers (task removal, failure reset, idle checks, polling, scheduler errors).
pt-BR rolling label typo: Sortendo → Sorteando.
pt-BR locale: standardized Poeira terminology in Hunt Analyzer and Better Yasir; removed unused Hunt Analyzer confirm/clearData keys.
NPC conversations stay English-only (e.g. King Tibianus starter-coin reply); quest UI strings remain localized.

Battle log filters split into ally and villain rows (dmg, heal, stats, status, pathing) with grid-aligned chips; toolbar actions are "All Filters" and "Clear Log". Units tab status chips are text-only; status detection reads live render data with merge rules for common effect variants.

Characters → Rankings loads faster: table skeleton appears immediately, duplicate tab fetch removed, rankings cache kept when reopening Cyclopedia, and rankings API requests no longer wait behind lower-priority queue jobs.
Benign duration passives (e.g. Jarred, Ultimate Healing, attack speed buffs) now appear on the Units tab and battle log.

Chrome MV3 service worker: background no longer mis-detects the browser or calls dynamic import() in the service worker (hardcoded registry fallbacks instead). Fixed message-port warnings from async onMessage handlers and added getGameLocalStorage bridge for Autoseller config.
